data.title: Brazil’s Biggest Bank Recommends Bitcoin Allocation of Up to 3%
data.description:
Itaú Unibanco, the largest private bank in Brazil and all of Latin America, has taken another major step toward institutional crypto adoption by formally recommending that clients allocate a portion of their portfolios to Bitcoin. The guidance comes from Itaú Asset, the bank’s asset management arm overseeing roughly $185 billion in assets.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/brazils-biggest-bank-recommends-bitcoin-allocation-of-up-to-3/

data.title: AI predicts XRP price if ETF inflows will hit $10 billion
data.description:
XRP exchange-traded funds (ETF) are posting one of the fastest adoption rates seen in the cryptocurrency ETF market, with cumulative inflows nearing $1 billion within just three to four weeks of launch. The pace exceeds early inflow trends recorded by Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) ETFs, signaling strong institutional appetite for XRP-linked investment products. Flow data shows inflows have remained consistently positive, with no notable net outflows reported so far. Meanwhile, XRP has continued to trade near the $2 level, a key psychological zone
source: https://finbold.com/ai-predicts-xrp-price-if-etf-inflows-will-hit-10-billion/

data.title: Rising deficits, inflation and fiscal disputes push US borrowing costs toward multi‑year highs
data.description:
A long stretch of high yields in long-term bonds is lifting borrowing costs across the world, and the United States is carrying one of the heaviest loads. Investors want extra pay to hold government debt because they see big deficits, firm inflation, and louder fights over central bank independence. Yields on long-dated debt have climbed […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/deficits-inflation-fiscal-us-borrowing-costs/

data.title: Tether’s Bid to Buy Italian Soccer Club Juventus Rejected by Majority Shareholder Exor
data.description:
Tether, the company best known for its popular stablecoin USDT, has made a bold attempt to acquire the renowned Italian soccer club Juventus; however, this proposal has been firmly rejected by Exor, the majority shareholder of the club. Exor, which is controlled by the Agnelli family, holds a significant stake in Juventus and has decided not to entertain Tether’s offer, opting to maintain its current ownership and management structure for the time being. This decision highlights the complexities and competitive nature of sports club ownership in Europe, as well as the challenges facing cryptocurrency companies looking to expand their influence in traditional sectors like professional sports.
source: https://www.coindesk.com/coindesk-news/2025/12/13/tether-s-bid-to-buy-italian-soccer-club-juventus-rejected-by-majority-shareholder-exor

data.title: Wall Street’s Jamie Dimon backs Kevin Warsh for Fed chair
data.description:
JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon on Thursday told a closed-door group of asset-management chiefs in New York that he supports Kevin Warsh to lead the Federal Reserve. Jamie said he believes Kevin Hassett would likely support Donald Trump’s call for faster rate cuts if he becomes chair, but Warsh would be harder to bend. That closed-door […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/jamie-dimon-backs-kevin-warsh-for-fed-chair/

data.title: Bitcoin: Here Is Why 50% Gains Might Be Next
data.description:
Bitcoin’s market structure is flashing a rare signal that previously preceded a powerful upside move. A key capitulation metric, often associated with extreme selling pressure and market exhaustion, has reached a new all-time high. The last time this indicator spiked to comparable levels, Bitcoin rallied roughly 50% shortly afterward
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-here-is-why-50-gains-might-be-next/
